references.text: Antonio, MA & IE Buot Jr. 2021. Characterization and delineation of two infraspecific taxa of Dioscorea esculenta (Lour.) Burkill: The leaf architecture approach. Biodiversitas 22: 1783-1789
Burkill, IH. 1951. Dioscoreaceae. Flora Malesiana series 1, 4: 293-336
Caddick, LR & P Wilkin. 1998. A revision of the genus Stenomeris (Dioscoreaceae). Kew Bulletin 53: 703-712.
Hsu, KM, JL Tsai, MY Chen, HM Ku & SC Liu. 2013. Molecular phylogeny of Dioscorea (Dioscoreaceae) in East and Southeast Asia. Blumea 58: 21-27
Kishor, KC, RS Pagare & W Arisdason. 2021. Typification of 18 Old World taxa in Dioscorea (Dioscoreaceae). Kew Bulletin 76: 339-363.
Pagare, RS & W Arisdason. 2016. Lectotypification in the genus Dioscorea L. (Dioscoreaceae). Webbia 71: 73-81.

species.distribution: Bangladesh, Bismarck Arch, Borneo, Cambodia, India, Java, Lesser Sunda Isls, Malay Peninsula, Myanmar, Nepal, New Guinea, Philippines, Sulawesi, Sumatra, Thailand, Vietnam. PALAWAN, PANAY (photos).
species.notes: Ube.

species.citation: L. , Sp. Pl., 1st Ed. (1753) 1033 ; Merr., Fl. Manila (1912) 151; Merrill, EPFP 1 (1923) 215; Burkill, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 4 (1951) 311. var. bulbifera. Distribution: Africa, Andaman Isls, Australia, Bangladesh, Borneo, Cambodia, China, India, Indian Ocean, Japan, Java, Korea, Laos, Lesser Sunda Isls, Malay Peninsula, Myanmar, Nepal, New Guinea, Pacific Ocean, Pakistan, Philippines, Ryukyu Isls, Sri Lanka, Sulawesi, Sumatra, Taiwan, Thailand, Vietnam. LEYTE, LUZON: Bataan, La Union, Mountain Province, Nueva Ecija, Rizal, Sorsogon, Zambales, MASBATE, MINDANAO. Notes: Thickets. 0-1000(-2700)m. Native.
species.distribution: Africa, Andaman Isls, Australia, Bangladesh, Borneo, Cambodia, China, India, Indian Ocean, Japan, Java, Korea, Laos, Lesser Sunda Isls, Malay Peninsula, Myanmar, Nepal, New Guinea, Pacific Ocean, Pakistan, Philippines, Ryukyu Isls, Sri Lanka, Sulawesi, Sumatra, Taiwan, Thailand, Vietnam. BICAWAYAN, BOHOL, CAMIGUIN, CATANDUANES, LEYTE, LUZON: Albay, Bataan, Batangas, Bulacan, Ifugao, Ilocos Norte, Kalinga, La Union, Laguna, Mountain Province, NCR, Nueva Ecija, Rizal, Sorsogon, Zambales, MASBATE, MINDANAO: Agusan del Norte, Agusan del Sur, Davao del Sur, Zamboanga del Norte, MINDORO, PALAWAN, PANAY, TABLAS.

species.citation: Prain & Burkill , J. Proc. Asiat. Soc. Bengal 4 (1908) 449 ; Merrill, EPFP 1 (1923) 216; Burkill, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 4 (1951) 317; Kishor et al., Kew Bull. 76 (2022) 342; Type: Cuming 1469 (K, lecto; K, P, isolecto), Luzon: Batangas, 1841. = Dioscorea echinata R.Knuth , Pflanzenr. (Engler) IV (43) (1924) 148 ; Merrill, EPFP 4 (1926) 242. = Dioscorea inaequifolia Elmer ex Prain & Burkill , LPB 5 (1913) 1595; Type: Elmer 10654 (NY, lecto; BISH, CAS, E, F, GH, K, L, US, isolecto), Mindanao: Davao, Mt Apo, May-1909. var. cumingii. Distribution: LUZON: Apayao, Bataan, Benguet, Camarines, Laguna, Mountain Province, Nueva Vizcaya, Rizal, Sorsogon, MINDANAO, MINDORO. Notes: Thickets and forests at low and medium elevation, ascending to 1400m. Native. var. polyphylla (R.Knuth) Burkill, Fl. Males., 1st Ser. 4 (1951) 317; ≡ Dioscorea polyphylla R.Knuth, Pflanzenr. (Engler) IV (43) (1924) 148. Distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. LUZON: Rizal, Zambales (photos). Native. var. ramosii Burkill, ined.; Burkill, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 4 (1951) 317. Distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. BATAN, CAMIGUIN DE BABUYANES. Notes: Burkill hints that this taxon may be conspecific with Dioscorea heptaphylla Sasaki of Taiwan. Native.
species.distribution: Philippines, Taiwan. BATAN, CAMIGUIN DE BABUYANES, LEYTE, LUZON: Albay, Apayao, Aurora, Bataan, Benguet, Camarines, Ilocos Sur, Isabela, Laguna, Mountain Province, Nueva Vizcaya, Quezon, Rizal, Sorsogon, Zambales (photos), MINDANAO: Davao del Sur, Misamis Oriental (photos), Zamboanga del Norte, MINDORO, NEGROS, SAMAR.

species.citation: Blanco , Fl. Filip., 1st Ed. (1837) 797 ; Merrill, EPFP 1 (1923) 216; Burkill, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 4 (1951) 332. = Dioscorea oxyphylla R.Knuth , Pflanzenr. (Engler) IV (43) (1924) 269 ; Merrill, EPFP 4 (1926) 242. = Dioscorea soror Prain & Burkill , LPB 5 (1913) 1598 ; Merrill, EPFP 1 (1923) 219. = Dioscorea soror var. glauca Prain & Burkill , J. Proc. Asiat. Soc. Bengal 10 (1914) 34 ; Merrill, EPFP 1 (1923) 219.
species.distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. CATANDUANES, CEBU, LUZON: Albay, Bataan, Bulacan, Cagayan, Ilocos Norte, Laguna, Mountain Province, NCR, Pampanga, Pangasinan, Quezon, Rizal, Zambales, MARINDUQUE, PALAWAN, PANAY.
species.notes: Low and medium elevation thickets and secondary forests.

species.citation: Prain & Burkill , LPB 5 (1913) 1594 ; Merrill, EPFP 1 (1923) 216; Burkill, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 4 (1951) 317; Type: Elmer 9156 (K, lecto; CAL, GH, isolecto), Luzon: Lucban, Tayabas Island, May-1907.
species.distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. BATANES, LUZON: Laguna, Quezon, SAMAR.
species.notes: Everwet forests. 0-800m.

species.citation: (Lour.) Burkill , Gard. Bull. Straits Settlem. 1 (1917) 396 ; Merrill, EPFP 1 (1923) 216; Burkill, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 4 (1951) 307; ≡ Oncus esculentus Lour. , Fl. Cochinch. (1790) 194. var. fasciculata (Roxb.) R.Knuth, Pflanzenr. (Engler) IV (43) (1924) 190; Antonio & Buot, Biodiversitas 22 (2021) 1787; ≡ Dioscorea aculeata var. fasciculata (Roxb.) Prain & Burkill, J. Proc. Asiat. Soc. Bengal 10 (1914) 20; ≡ Dioscorea fasciculata Roxb., Fl. Ind. (Roxburgh) 2 (1832) 801; Merrill, EPFP 1 (1923) 216. Notes: Plants devoid of thorny roots; entirely cultivated. Naturalized. var. spinosa (Roxb. ex Prain & Burkill) R.Knuth, Pflanzenr. (Engler) IV (43) (1924) 189; Antonio & Buot, Biodiversitas 22 (2021) 1787; ≡ Dioscorea aculeata var. spinosa Roxb. ex Prain & Burkill, J. Proc. Asiat. Soc. Bengal 10 (1914) 20; ≡ Dioscorea spinosa Roxb. ex Hook.f., Fl. Brit. India 6 (1892) 291, nom. illeg., non Burm. Distribution: PANAY (photos). Notes: Plants well provided with thorny roots; includes both cultigens and wild forms. 0-5m (photos). Cryptogenic.
species.distribution: Bangladesh, Bismarck Arch, Borneo, Cambodia, India, Java, Lesser Sunda Isls, Malay Peninsula, Moluccas, Myanmar, Nepal, New Guinea, Philippines, Sulawesi, Sumatra, Thailand, Vietnam. BATANES, LUZON: Bataan, Benguet, Bulacan, Cagayan, La Union, Laguna, Nueva Vizcaya, Pangasinan, Rizal, PANAY (photos), SEMIRARA.
species.notes: Low elevation thickets and secondary forests. Also often cultivated. 0-5m (photos).

species.citation: Blume , Enum. Pl. Javae (1827) 22 ; Merrill, EPFP 1 (1923) 217; Burkill, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 4 (1951) 329.
species.distribution: Borneo, Java, Lesser Sunda Isls, Malay Peninsula, Moluccas, Philippines, Sulawesi, Sumatra, Thailand. BALABAC, BOHOL, CULION, JOLO, LUZON: Bataan, Batangas, Laguna, NCR, Nueva Ecija, Rizal, MINDANAO: Zamboanga, PALAWAN.
species.notes: Low elevation thickets and ravines.

species.citation: Prain & Burkill , LPB 5 (1913) 1593 ; Merrill, EPFP 1 (1923) 217; Burkill, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 4 (1951) 309; Type: Elmer 9095 (B, lecto; CAL, isolecto), Luzon: Tayabas.
species.distribution: Borneo, Pacific Ocean, Philippines. BATAN, BUYAYAO (photos), CAMIGUIN DE BABUYANES, CATANDUANES, LUZON: Albay, Aurora, Ifugao, Isabela, Laguna, Quezon, Sorsogon, MINDANAO: South Cotabato, MINDORO, PALAWAN, PANAY (photos).
species.notes: Low and medium elevation humid forests. 8m (photos).

species.citation: Prain & Burkill , J. Proc. Asiat. Soc. Bengal 10 (1914) 35 ; Merrill, EPFP 1 (1923) 217; Burkill, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 4 (1951) 328; Type: Loher 7017 (K, lecto), Luzon: Rizal, Montalban.
species.distribution: New Guinea, Philippines. BILIRAN, LUZON: Rizal.
species.notes: No Philippine data on habitat and elevation, but in New Guinea reported in Agathis forest. 300m.

species.citation: Dennst. , Schlüssel Hortus Malab. (1818) 33 ; Merrill, EPFP 1 (1923) 217; Burkill, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 4 (1951) 318; Raganas et al., Biodiversitas 26 (2025) 1194. var. hispida. Distribution: Andaman Isls, Australia, Bangladesh, Bismarck Arch, Borneo, Cambodia, China, India, Java, Lesser Sunda Isls, Malay Peninsula, Moluccas, Myanmar, Nepal, New Guinea, Philippines, Sulawesi, Sumatra, Taiwan, Thailand, Tibet, Vietnam. Notes: Throughout the Philippines in thickets and regrowth forests at low and medium elevation. Native. var. daemona (Roxb.) Prain & Burkill, Ann. Roy. Bot. Gard. (Calcutta) 14 (1936) 192. Distribution: ?Philippines.
species.distribution: Andaman Isls, Australia, Bangladesh, Bismarck Arch, Borneo, Cambodia, China, India, Java, Lesser Sunda Isls, Malay Peninsula, Moluccas, Myanmar, Nepal, New Guinea, Philippines, Sulawesi, Sumatra, Taiwan, Thailand, Tibet, Vietnam. APULIT, BILIRAN, CULION, DONDONAY, LEYTE, LUZON: Albay, Benguet, Bulacan, Cagayan, Laguna, Nueva Vizcaya, Pangasinan, Quezon, Rizal, MINDANAO: Agusan del Norte, Bukidnon, Davao del Norte, Davao del Sur, Lanao del Sur, Misamis Oriental (photos), South Cotabato (photos), MINDORO, NEGROS, PALAWAN, PANAY, SAMAR.
species.notes: Sea level and low and medium elevation. 0-300m (photos).

species.citation: Prain & Burkill , J. Proc. Asiat. Soc. Bengal 10 (1914) 33 ; Merrill, EPFP 1 (1923) 217; Burkill, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 4 (1951) 332; Kishor et al., Kew Bull. 76 (2022) 348; Type: Loher 1886 (K, lecto; CAL, P, M, isolecto), Luzon: San Francisco del Monte (Manila), 16-Oct-1890.
species.distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. CEBU, ?LEYTE (photos), LUZON: Benguet, NCR, Rizal, MINDANAO: Davao Oriental, PALAWAN.
species.notes: Low elevation thickets. According to Merrill (1922) perhaps referable to Dioscorea divaricata Blanco.

species.citation: Schauer , Nov. Actorum Acad. Caes. Leop.-Carol. Nat. Cur. 19(Suppl. 1) (1843) 444 ; Merrill, EPFP 1 (1923) 218; Burkill, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 4 (1951) 325.
species.distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. DALUPIRI, LUZON: Apayao, Batangas, Bulacan, Ifugao, Ilocos Norte, Ilocos Sur, La Union, Laguna, Mountain Province, NCR, Nueva Vizcaya, Quezon, Rizal, Sorsogon, Zambales, MINDORO, PALAWAN.
species.notes: Chiefly at low and medium elevation thickets. Common in the provinces near Manila where there are two drier breaks in the year. Fl. Sep or earlier.

species.citation: Prain & Burkill , LPB 5 (1913) 1598 ; Merrill, EPFP 1 (1923) 218; Burkill, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 4 (1951) 332; Kishor et al., Kew Bull. 76 (2022) 348; Type: Elmer 11924 (K, lecto; BISH, BM, CAL, isolecto), Mindanao: Todaya, Mt Apo, Oct-1909.
species.distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. MINDANAO: Davao, Davao del Sur, MINDORO.
species.notes: Old clearings and thickets, c. 700m.

species.citation: Lam. , Encycl. (Lamarck) 3 (1789) 231 ; Merrill, EPFP 1 (1923) 218; Burkill, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 4 (1951) 331. = Dioscorea nummularia var. glauca Prain & Burkill , J. Proc. Asiat. Soc. Bengal 10 (1914) 35 ; Merrill, EPFP 1 (1923) 218.
species.distribution: Andaman Isls, Bismarck Arch, Borneo, Java, Moluccas, New Guinea, Philippines, Sulawesi, Vietnam. BALABAC, BASILAN, CAMIGUIN, CAMOTES, CEBU, LEYTE, LUZON: Aurora, Cagayan, Camarines Norte, Ifugao, Ilocos Norte, Ilocos Sur, Isabela, La Union, Laguna, Mountain Province, Pampanga, Quezon, Sorsogon, MINDANAO: Davao, Davao del Sur, Surigao, Zamboanga Sibugay, Zamboanga del Norte, MINDORO, PALAWAN, POLILLO, SAMAR.
species.notes: In most or all provinces in Luzon. Low and medium elevation thickets.

species.citation: Prain & Burkill , Bull. Misc. Inform. Kew 1925 (1925) 59 ; Merrill, EPFP 4 (1926) 242; Burkill, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 4 (1951) 305; Kishor et al., Kew Bull. 76 (2022) 355; Type: Merrill 9584 (US, lecto; K, SING, isolecto), Palawan: Silanga, May-1913.
species.distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. PALAWAN, PANAY.

species.citation: L. , Sp. Pl., 1st Ed. (1753) 1032 ; Merr., Fl. Manila (1912) 152; Merrill, EPFP 1 (1923) 218; Burkill, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 4 (1951) 315. = Dioscorea pentaphylla var. malaica Prain & Burkill , J. Proc. Asiat. Soc. Bengal 10 (1914) 23. = Dioscorea pentaphylla var. palmata Burkill , Gard. Bull. Straits Settlem. 3 (1924) 258.
species.distribution: Australia, Bangladesh, Borneo, Cambodia, China, India, Indian Ocean, Java, Laos, Lesser Sunda Isls, Malay Peninsula, Moluccas, Myanmar, Nepal, New Guinea, Pacific Ocean, Philippines, Ryukyu Isls, Sri Lanka, Sulawesi, Sumatra, Taiwan, Thailand, Tibet, Vietnam. BATANES, BUSUANGA, LUZON: Aurora, Bataan, Benguet, Camarines Sur, Laguna, NCR, Nueva Vizcaya, Rizal, MINDANAO: Agusan del Norte, MINDORO, NEGROS, PALAWAN, PANAY, SAMAR.
species.notes: 340m (photos).

species.citation: Prain & Burkill , LPB 5 (1913) 1597 ; Merrill, EPFP 1 (1923) 219; Burkill, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 4 (1951) 325; Type: Elmer 6399 (NYBG, lecto; CAL, isolecto), Luzon: Benguet, Twin-peaks, May-1904. var. peperoides. = Dioscorea peperoides var. sagittifolia Prain & Burkill, J. Proc. Asiat. Soc. Bengal 10 (1914) 28; Merrill, EPFP 1 (1923) 219. Distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. GOLO, LUZON: Benguet, Nueva Vizcaya, Rizal, PALAWAN. Notes: Thickets. In Palawan occurring on limestone formations at sea level. 0-1300m. Native.
species.distribution: Philippines, Vietnam. AMBIL, GOLO, LUZON: Benguet, Ilocos Sur, Nueva Vizcaya, Rizal, MINDORO, PABELLON ISLS, PALAWAN.

species.citation: R.Knuth , Pflanzenr. (Engler) IV (43) (1924) 271 ; Merrill, EPFP 1 (1924) 242.
species.distribution: Endemic to the Philippines.
species.notes: Burkill (1951): “The name is virtually a nomen nudum, the scrap of which it was given being inadequate even for the establishment of the section. But it is interesting to know that a Dioscorea occurs on the E slopes of Mt Apo in Mindanao (S Philippines) at that considerable height of 2000m”.

species.citation: Uline ex R.Knuth , Pflanzenr. (Engler) IV (43) (1924) 271 ; Merrill, EPFP 4 (1926) 242.
species.distribution: LUZON: Laguna.
species.notes: Known from Wilkes Expedition s.n., Laguna, Los Baños. Apparently unaccounted for in Burkill (1951). Synonym of Dioscorea loheri Prain & Burkill?

species.citation: Oliv. , Hooker's Icon. Pl. 24 (1894) t.2328 ; Burkill, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 4 (1951) 297; Romeroso et al., J. Asia-Pacific Biodiv. 17 (2024) 10; Type: from Borneo. = Stenomeris mindanaensis R.Knuth , Pflanzenr. (Engler) IV (43) (1924) 345 ; Merrill, EPFP 1 (1924) 242; Caddick & Wilkin, Kew Bull. 53 (1998) 710; Type: Merrill 7301 (A or NY, holo; K, L, iso), Mindanao: Butuan, Agusan River, 1910.
species.distribution: Borneo, Malay Peninsula, Philippines, Sumatra. HOMONHON, MINDANAO: Agusan, Agusan del Norte, Davao, Davao Oriental, Surigao, Surigao del Norte (photos), Zamboanga, Zamboanga Sibugay, Zamboanga del Norte (photos).
species.notes: On wet or sandy soil. Lowland. 400-500m (photos).

species.citation: Planch. , Ann. Sci. Nat. (Paris), 3rd Sér. 18 (1852) 320 ; Merrill, EPFP 1 (1923) 219; Burkill, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 4 (1951) 295; Caddick & Wilkin, Kew Bull. 53 (1998) 709; Type: Cuming 875 (K, holo), Luzon: Albay. = Stenomeris cumingiana Becc. , Nuovo Giorn. Bot. Ital. 2 (1870) 8 ; Merrill, EPFP 1 (1923) 219; Type: Cuming 1739 (FI, holo), Philippines: 1841. = Stenomeris wallisii Taub. , Bot. Jahrb. Syst. 15(Beibl. 38) (1893) 2 ; Merrill, EPFP 1 (1923) 220; Type: Wallis (B, holo), Philippines.
species.distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. ALABAT, BILIRAN, CATANDUANES, LEYTE, LUZON: Albay, Aurora, Cagayan, Camarines Norte, Isabela, Laguna, Quezon, Rizal, Sorsogon, MINDANAO: Agusan del Norte, Surigao, Zamboanga Sibugay, MINDORO, ROMBLON, SAMAR (photos), SIBUYAN.
